---
title: "Kick Off with Shiny for Python"
subtitle: "AthlyticZ Masterclass &mdash; 3 July 2026"
author: "Veerle van Leemput"
format:
  revealjs:
    brand: ../_brand.yml
    theme: default
    transition: fade
    highlight-style: github
    code-line-numbers: false
    code-block-height: 400px
    slide-number: true
    footer: "AthlyticZ — Kick Off with Shiny for Python"
---

## Python has a lot of app frameworks {.center}

| Framework | Reactivity | Best for |
|-----------|-----------|----------|
| **Streamlit** | Reruns top-to-bottom on every interaction | Quick data apps, ML demos |
| **Dash** | Explicit callback decorators linking inputs to outputs | Analytical dashboards, Plotly-heavy apps |
| **Gradio** | Event-driven, limited layout control | ML model demos |
| **Shiny** | Fine-grained: only what changed reruns | Production apps, complex state |

---

## Why Shiny?

- **Reactive by design** — inputs, outputs, and calculations form a dependency graph. Only what needs to update, updates.
- **Production-ready** — Posit-backed, Shiny for R has been in production for 10+ years. Python got it in 2022.
- **Full layout control** — Bootstrap-based UI, cards, sidebars, navbars, theming. Not just widgets stacked vertically.
- **One framework, two languages** — if you ever touch Shiny for R, you already know 80% of Shiny for Python.

---

## Two ways to write Shiny {.center}

Same reactive engine. Two different styles.

|  | **Core** | **Express** |
|--|----------|-------------|
| Structure | Explicit UI + server | Everything at the top level |
| Layout | Nested function calls | `with` context managers |
| Outputs | 2 steps: placeholder + render fn | 1 step: render fn places itself |
| Best for | Larger apps, complex layouts | Quick builds, demos |

This session uses **Core** — easier to see what's happening.

---

## Core: explicit structure

```python
from shiny import App, ui, render

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_text("name", "Your name"),
    ui.output_text("greeting"),   # step 1: placeholder in UI
)

def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    def greeting():               # step 2: render fn in server
        return f"Hello, {input.name()}!"

app = App(app_ui, server)
```

Two steps for every output. The wiring is explicit.

---

## Express: less boilerplate

```python
from shiny.express import input, render, ui

with ui.card():
    ui.input_text("name", "Your name")

    @render.text
    def greeting():               # defined once, placed here
        return f"Hello, {input.name()}!"
```

No `App()`. No separate server function. The render decorator places the output where you define it.

::: {.callout-note}
Source: [shiny.posit.co/py/docs/express-vs-core.html](https://shiny.posit.co/py/docs/express-vs-core.html)
:::

---

## What we're building

A World Cup 2026 group standings dashboard.

- Pick a group (A through L)
- See the four teams, sorted by points
- v1: plain, functional
- v2: branded, polished — same `_brand.yml` that's powering these slides

The app talks to a real REST API with live tournament data.

---

## The API

Base URL: `https://worldcup26.ir`

```
GET /get/group/?name=A
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```

```json
{
  "group": "A",
  "teams": [
    { "team_id": "1", "pts": "3", "gf": "2", "ga": "0" },
    { "team_id": "2", "pts": "3", "gf": "1", "ga": "1" },
    { "team_id": "3", "pts": "0", "gf": "0", "ga": "2" },
    { "team_id": "4", "pts": "0", "gf": "0", "ga": "1" }
  ]
}
```

Register once, get a token that lasts 84 days. Store it in `.env`.

---

## What is uv?

**uv** is a fast Python package and project manager — one tool that replaces `pip`, `venv`, and `pip-tools`.

- Written in Rust → 10–100× faster than pip
- Manages virtual environments for you automatically
- Locks exact dependency versions in `uv.lock`
- Made by Astral (the folks behind Ruff)

Install it once:

```bash
pip install uv
```

---

## Setting up a project with uv

**Initialize the project:**

```bash
uv init
```

The first time you run `uv run`, `uv sync`, or `uv lock`, uv also creates:

- `.venv/` — virtual environment in your project root
- `uv.lock` — lockfile pinning exact versions

**Add your dependencies:**

```bash
uv add requests pandas shiny
```

**Lock them:**

```bash
uv lock
```

::: {.callout-note}
In **Positron**, the `!` prefix matters: Console pane (Python interpreter) → `!uv init`, `!uv add …`
Terminal pane → no `!` needed
:::

---

## Project setup

Three commands to go from zero to running app:

```bash
uv init my-app
cd my-app
uv add "shiny[theme]" requests pandas python-dotenv
```

Run the app:

```bash
shiny run app.py --reload
```

That's it. `uv` handles the virtual env, the lockfile, everything.

---

## Let's go! {.center}

Open Positron. We're starting from a blank file.

```
app/
├── v1_basics.py    ← we build this together
├── v2_polished.py  ← brand.yml, value_box, styled table
└── .env            ← your API token goes here
```
